config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja oleh kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak-hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan atas nama "kepentingan umum". Di Indonesia, saya melihat pentingnya gotong royong dan kepentingan bersama, tetapi itu tidak boleh dijadikan alasan untuk mengorbankan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa meniadakan hak individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should routinely take priority over individual rights, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to excuse abuse, discrimination, and government overreach. Public safety and the common good matter, but they do not justify treating individual rights as optional, especially rights like free speech, due process, and equal protection. If society can override the individual too easily, then the whole point of those rights is lost. The better standard is that society can place limited, narrowly tailored limits on rights only when there is a clear and serious need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, ketidakadilan, atau pengabaian hak orang kecil, padahal hak individu justru batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Gotong royong dan kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh perlindungan yang jelas terhadap kebebasan, martabat, dan proses hukum setiap orang. Jadi, kalaupun ada pembatasan, itu harus sangat sempit, transparan, dan benar-benar bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan dalih untuk mengorbankan individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that idea has repeatedly been used to justify overreach and abuses against unpopular people. The strongest version of the common-good argument is not that rights disappear, but that some rights can face narrow limits when there is a real emergency or a direct harm to others. That is why due process, free speech, and equal protection matter so much: they are the guardrails that keep “the public interest” from becoming an excuse for arbitrary power. If society can override the individual too easily, then rights are no longer rights, just permissions granted by whoever is in charge.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus menjadi prioritas umum di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya melihat sendiri betapa sering frasa seperti “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk menekan suara yang berbeda, mengabaikan warga kecil, atau melegalkan tindakan yang sebenarnya tidak adil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dijaga lewat batas yang kuat seperti due process, kebebasan dasar, dan perlindungan terhadap penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Jadi bagi saya, masyarakat tidak boleh diberi kuasa untuk menghapus hak individu, hanya untuk membatasinya secara sangat ketat dan benar-benar perlu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American perspective, that kind of claim has too often been used to silence dissent, excuse discrimination, and let authorities treat people as expendable in the name of the common good. Public needs matter, but rights like due process, free speech, and equal protection are exactly what stop “society” from becoming a cover for abuse. I would only accept very limited restrictions when there is a clear, concrete harm and the limits are tightly justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
